t_ch: 红方选手，熔锤镇的卢卡——你准备好了吗？
t_en: In the red corner, Luka from Moltammerville — are you ready?

t_ch: …随时随地！
t_en: ...Whenever you are!

t_ch: …好。无限制级别搏击，一局一胜制，以一方选手失去比赛意愿、或裁判判罚视为对局结束……
t_en: ...Okay. This is a no holds barred contest. One round only. Victory will be declared when one contestant submits, or the fight is stopped by the referee...

t_ch: …第一回合，开始！
t_en: Alright... fight!

t_ch: ——「见识下火花四溅吧！」
t_en: Let's see some sparks fly!

t_ch: 我…还不能……
t_en: I... can't...

t_ch: 「地火」的卢卡，不论你前来的动机是什么——
t_en: Luka of Wildfire, it does not matter why you came here...

t_ch: ——我都承认你的强大。你的表现值得让我重新评估人类的极限。
t_en: ...I acknowledge your strength. Your performance was enough to make me re-evaluate the limits of humanity.

t_ch: 哈…哈……
t_en: Ha... ha...

t_ch: 那还…真是……
t_en: That's... really...

t_ch: 喂，醒醒！别倒下，快站起来啊！
t_en: Hey, wake up! On your feet!

t_ch: 卢卡！喂，卢卡——！
t_en: Luka! Hey, Luka...!

t_ch: 「小飞」和「大奔」就拜托你们了！
t_en: Please, I'm depending on you for Little Fay and Big Ben!

t_ch: 它们可是我毕生的心血……
t_en: They are the fruits of my entire life's labor...

t_ch: 我、我认输！求你了，别再找我麻烦了！
t_en: I—I'm sorry! Please, I've learned my lesson!

t_ch: 这机器人的系统我已经帮你们重置好了，它现在不会攻击你们了！赶紧把它带走吧，带它回原来的主人那儿！
t_en: I've reset its systems — it won't attack you anymore! Just get it out of here — take it back to its owner!

t_ch: 呜…这地方是待不下去了，接下来怎么办……
t_en: Ugh... I can't stay here anymore... What am I gonna do...

t_ch: 卢卡——你经常帮人找东西吗？
t_en: Luka... Do you often find stuff for other people?

t_ch: 算是吧？小孩子们丢了东西总会来找我。
t_en: Seems like it. Kids always come to me when they lose stuff.

t_ch: 嗯哼…怪不得。
t_en: Ha... no wonder.

t_ch: 真破败…我可不想住在这儿啊。
t_en: Man, this is area is so run-down... I wouldn't wanna live here.

t_ch: 这里的人也不想，但他们没得选。
t_en: The people living here don't want to either, they've got no other choice.

t_ch: 我们可以改变这些，对吧？
t_en: We can change that, right?

t_ch: 嗯。
t_en: Yeah.

t_ch: 看！那边的家伙，鬼鬼祟祟的——
t_en: Look! That fella over there is acting pretty shifty.

t_ch: ——肯定就是咱们要找的小贼。这下可有的聊了！
t_en: He's gotta be the thief we're looking for. Let's go and have a chat with him!

t_ch: 一切顺利……
t_en: Smooth as you like...

t_ch: 卡西迪，你也有今天……
t_en: You had it coming, Cassidy...

t_ch: 卢卡，你老实交代——你来这里是不是有什么别的目的？
t_en: Luka, come clean with me. Are you here for some other reason?

t_ch: 只是修炼，真的！这么多疑可不像你啊，希儿。
t_en: It's just training, for real! It's not like you to be so suspicious, Seele.

t_ch: 对了，你们是怎么跟史瓦罗和解的？说说细节呗。
t_en: Say, how did you settle your beef with Svarog? Tell me the juicy details.

t_ch: 说来话长…还是等下次吧。
t_en: It's a long story... I'll tell you later.

t_ch: 卢卡，你……
t_en: Luka, you—

t_ch: 嗯？怎么了？
t_en: Hmm? What's up?

t_ch: …算了，和你说了也白说。
t_en: ...Never mind. I get the feeling I'm wasting my breath.

t_ch: 去找史瓦罗的事，你跟头儿打过招呼了？
t_en: Have you talked to Oleg about going to see Svarog?

t_ch: 哈哈哈，你就想问这个？我也不是什么事都跟师傅说的。
t_en: Hahaha, that's all you wanna ask? I don't necessarily tell master Oleg everything, you know.
*sigh*

t_ch: 卢卡，你……
t_en: Luka, make sure you—

t_ch: 咳…算了。你量力而为。
t_en: *sigh* Never mind. Just don't go overboard.

t_ch: 卢卡，加油！
t_en: Go, Luka!

t_ch: 史瓦罗，加油！
t_en: Go, Svarog!

t_ch: 哈哈哈，我真看不出来你是支持哪边的！
t_en: Hahaha, I really can't tell whose side you're on!

t_ch: 投降吧，你不是我们的对手！
t_en: Give it up! You're no match for us!

t_ch: 我…老子没退路了！
t_en: I—I can't turn back now!

t_ch: 有点骨气——那就再吃我两拳！
t_en: You've got guts... Time to end this!

t_ch: 还差一点！那家伙要撑不住了。
t_en: Nearly there! It's not gonna last much longer.

t_ch: 哈，我来给它致命一击！
t_en: Ha! I'll deal the final blow!

t_ch: 哈，终于有个能抗住这一拳的对手了！
t_en: Ha! Finally, an opponent that can take the punches head-on!

t_ch: 有力的一击——但击不穿我的防御。
t_en: A powerful strike — but not enough to break my defenses.

t_ch: 不愧是史瓦罗大佬——那再尝尝这招如何！
t_en: That's why they call you Boss Svarog! How about this!?

t_ch: 嘁…不赖嘛……
t_en: Tsk... not bad...

t_ch: …红方选手被击倒！十，九，八，七……
t_en: ...Red contestant down! Ten, nine, eight, seven...

t_ch: …怎么可能，离结束还早着呢！再来！
t_en: ...No way. I'm a long way from tapping out! Let's go!

t_ch: 就差…一点……
t_en: Just a bit... more...

t_ch: 胜负已分。
t_en: The winner has been decided.

t_ch: …怎么可能，离结束还早着呢！再来！
t_en: ...No way. I'm a long way from tapping out! Let's go!

t_ch: 就差…一点……
t_en: Just a bit... more...

t_ch: …够了，卢卡！就到此为止吧！
t_en: ...Enough, Luka! Let's end it here!

t_ch: …不…不行，还没完！
t_en: ...N—No! It's not over!

t_ch: 必须让你…看到……
t_en: I must... show you...

t_ch: 你让我印象深刻，「地火」的卢卡。
t_en: You make a strong impression, Luka of Wildfire.

t_ch: 我会记住这场战斗。
t_en: I will remember this fight.

t_ch: 哈…哈…多谢……
t_en: Ha... ha... thanks...

t_ch: 但是…还没…没完……
t_en: But... it's not... over...

t_ch: 看，他在那儿！
t_en: Look, there he is!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，走，我们跟上去。记得压低声音……
t_en: {NICKNAME}, let's follow him. Stay quiet...

t_ch: …哇！！荣誉队员，希儿姐——
t_en: ...Wow! Honorary member, Miss See—

t_ch: 嘘！别出声，你差点害我们暴露了！
t_en: Shh! Be quiet! You almost exposed us!

t_ch: 唔，你们偷偷摸摸的，到底在干嘛呀？
t_en: Umm, why are you sneaking around like this?

t_ch: 在寻宝。
t_en: We're treasure hunting.

t_ch: 噢！好刺激呀！
t_en: Wow! That's so exciting!

t_ch: 在找你。
t_en: We're looking for you.

t_ch: …噢？虎克有点糊涂…
t_en: ...Huh? Hook's confused...

t_ch: 在跟踪…
t_en: We're stalking—
—Hey!

t_ch: 再不快点就要跟丢了…虎克，你赶紧回镇上去，别在这儿晃悠了。
t_en: We're gonna lose him if we don't hurry... Hook, run back to town — stop hanging around here.

t_ch: …虎克知道啦，刚才卢卡哥哥也是这么说的！
t_en: *sigh* Okayyyy... That's what Luka said too.

t_ch: 希望你们两个能找到宝贝！
t_en: I hope you two find the treasure!

t_ch: 一个盒子？
t_en: A case?

t_ch: 我…不知怎的，我总感觉……
t_en: I... For some reason, I feel like...

t_ch: 不应该打开它？
t_en: ...We shouldn't open it?

t_ch: …嗯。没什么理由，纯粹是直觉。
t_en: ...Yeah. I don't have a reason. It's just a feeling.

t_ch: 可咱们跟了这一路…要是不打开看看，就白折腾一场了。
t_en: Then again, we followed him for ages... It'll be a waste if we don't see what's inside.

t_ch: 费了这么多力，必须打开看看。
t_en: We've come this far — let's open it up.

t_ch: …嗯。你说得对。
t_en: ...Yeah, you're right.

t_ch: 我来开吧，不知道里面到底装的什么——
t_en: I wonder what's in there. Here, let me open it...

t_ch: 这是…什么？笔记本？
t_en: Is this a... notebook?

t_ch: 看着像是小孩子的字迹，还有涂鸦…{NICKNAME}，你也来看看。
t_en: It looks like a kid's handwriting, and some scribbled art... {NICKNAME}, have a look.

t_ch: 卢卡哥哥，你好！
t_en: Mister Luka, hello!

t_ch: 我叫玛吉，和你一样来自熔锤镇，是你的忠实拳迷！虽然我从小身体不好，但还是梦想着有一天能跟你一样，成为擂台上的大明星！
t_en: My name is Margie, and I'm from Moltammerville too. I'm a big fan of yours! I've been sick since I was a baby, but I still dream of becoming a big star in the cage one day — just like you!

t_ch: 虽然有些难为情，但我还是想托爸爸把这个小本本送给你……
t_en: It's a bit embarrassing, but I wanted my Dad to give you this notebook...

t_ch: …因为我想让卢卡哥哥知道，我会一直以你为目标努力！而且，我还想把自己的愿望偷偷跟你分享…哪怕只是我的幻想！
t_en: ...I wanted to tell you that you've inspired me to keep working hard and achieve my goal, even if it's hard to reach!

t_ch: 等我成为和哥哥一样厉害的拳手，我一定要尽力帮助下层区的大家。
t_en: When I become a fighter as awesome as you, I'm gonna do my best to help people in the Underworld.

t_ch: 我想带着小伙伴一起，帮人们解决烦恼，看着他们露出可爱的笑脸。
t_en: I wanna team up with my friends and help everyone together so they can smile again.

t_ch: 听着他们感激的话，然后喊出我的决胜台词——
t_en: I'll listen to their thanks and then yell out my winning slogan:

t_ch: 「熔锤镇出身的勇士，无敌的磐岩冠军——」
t_en: "The Moltammerville warrior, the peerless Boulder Champion..."

t_ch: 「再次维护了公正跟和平！」
t_en: "...upholding justice and peace once again!"

t_ch: 然后，我还想把骚扰大家的坏人全都赶走——
t_en: Then, I wanna chase away all the bad things hurting everyone...

t_ch: 和小伙伴们一起，把那些吓人的怪物收拾得干干净净！
t_en: ...I'll go with my friends and clear out all the scary monsters!

t_ch: 帮助下层变得安全，然后喊出我的决胜台词——
t_en: I'll make the Underworld safe, then yell out my other winning slogan:

t_ch: 「熔锤出身的勇士，无敌的磐岩冠军——」
t_en: "The Moltammerville warrior, the peerless Boulder Champion..."

t_ch: 「让世界上的邪恶无处遁形！」
t_en: "...defeater of evil throughout the world!"

t_ch: 最后…我还想给下层区的大家争取更好的生活！
t_en: Lastly... I wanna make life better for the Underworld!

t_ch: 妈妈说，有个叫史瓦罗的家伙一直拦着我们，不准我们回上层区看看……
t_en: Mom said there's someone called Svarog who keeps stopping us from going back to the Overworld...

t_ch: 所以，我要去挑战那个大机器人，让他知道我这个拳王的厉害！
t_en: So I'm gonna challenge that big robot and show him what I can do as a champion fighter!

t_ch: 「可是，史瓦罗太强大了，我们肯定没有胜算！」小伙伴们听说以后，心里都敲起了退堂鼓。
t_en: "But Svarog is too strong! We'll never win!" My friends are gonna all wanna back out when they hear about this.

t_ch: 玛吉知道，必须给大家做出榜样，我要挺身而出，一个人去找史瓦罗较量。
t_en: But I know I have to be an example for everyone. I need to stick my neck out and go challenge Svarog on my own.

t_ch: 「咚！哐！嘭！」那场面真是激烈！最后我们打成了平手，谁都没法战胜对方。
t_en: "Bam! Crack! Boom!" It's gonna be such an epic fight! And then we'll reach a draw. Neither of us will be able to win.

t_ch: 不过，史瓦罗承认了玛吉的实力，也愿意听我讲讲下层区大家的事情。
t_en: But Svarog will acknowledge my strength and be willing to listen to the Underworlders.

t_ch: 结果皆大欢喜！小伙伴们鼓起了掌，受苦的人们也得到了解放。
t_en: So, it'll be a happy ending! My friends will clap, and all the people suffering will be free.

t_ch: 不过…这些都还只是玛吉的梦想，不知要过多久才能实现……
t_en: But... all that is just my dream. I dunno how long it's gonna be till it becomes reality...

t_ch: 我现在躺在病床上，但我一点都不害怕！因为有卢卡哥哥在，你的每一句鼓励我都记得清清楚楚。
t_en: I'm lying in bed sick right now, but as long as Mister Luka is around I won't be scared! I remember everything you said to encourage me.

t_ch: 我想，在成为被大家仰慕的冠军之前，我最先要战胜的就是这些病痛。
t_en: Before becoming a champion, I wanna defeat this illness first.

t_ch: 然后，我就要重新学会站立，行走，锻炼…直到实现所有这些愿望！
t_en: Then, I need to learn how to stand, walk, and exercise again... until I achieve all my dreams!

t_ch: 我希望…我相信，卢卡哥哥一定会支持我到最后的，对吗？
t_en: I hope... No, I believe Mister Luka will support me till the end, right?

t_ch: 原来如此…
t_en: So that's why...

t_ch: 卢卡，那家伙…还真是幼稚啊。
t_en: Luka... I think I get it now.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，我想…我想咱们还是忘了这事吧。你觉得呢？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, maybe we should... forget about all this. What do you say?

t_ch: 同意。
t_en: I agree.

t_ch: 嗯…也许再过一段时间，我们可以找他聊聊。
t_en: Mm... Maybe one day we can talk about it with him... but let's give him some time.

t_ch: 我想找他聊聊。
t_en: I kinda wanna talk to him about it.

t_ch: 嗯…我也是。但还是再等一段时间吧。
t_en: Mm... Me too... but maybe we should give him some time.

t_ch: 今天我们就先回镇上吧，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Let's head back to town for today, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 晚安…玛吉。
t_en: Good night... Margie.

t_ch: 希儿？{NICKNAME}？你们怎么在这儿？
t_en: Seele? {NICKNAME}? What are you guys doing here?

t_ch: 糟糕，露馅了……
t_en: Oh no, he caught us...

t_ch: 等等，那是…虎克？！她在这儿干嘛？
t_en: Wait, that's... Hook!? What's she doing here?

t_ch: 无处不在的鼹鼠党…
t_en: The Moles are everywhere...

t_ch: 这小鬼……
t_en: Up to no good...

t_ch: 指使卢卡的一定就是虎克！
t_en: So Hook was the mastermind behind all of this!

t_ch: …我都听不出你是不是在开玩笑了。
t_en: ...I can't tell if you're joking.

t_ch: 卢卡应该会劝她回镇上去吧？毕竟那家伙很在意小孩子的安全。
t_en: Luka should tell her to go back to town — he's always worrying after the kids' safety.

t_ch: …咱们都跟到这儿了，可不能让这小鬼把事情搞砸！
t_en: ...We've come this far, we can't let this kid ruin everything!

t_ch: 嘘，她要过来了！
t_en: Shh, she's coming this way!

t_ch: （裂界呼啸的声音）
t_en: (The sound of howling in the Fragmentum)

t_ch: （工具掉落的声音）
t_en: (The sound of tools clanking to the ground)

t_ch: 喂，太近了，退回来点！
t_en: Hey, that's too close! Step back a bit!

t_ch: 唔…他这是要去哪…？
t_en: Ugh... where is he going...?

t_ch: 不太对劲…喂，咱们先躲一下！
t_en: This doesn't feel right... Hey, let's hide for a bit!

t_ch: 好险…还好没跟丢。快，追上去！
t_en: Phew... thank goodness we didn't lose him. Quick, let's keep up the chase!

t_ch: 他刚才…好像放下了什么东西？
t_en: Did he... put something down?

t_ch: 走，我们过去看看。
t_en: Let's go have a look.

t_ch: 被跟踪目标发现了，行动失败…再尝试一次吧。
t_en: You were discovered by the target... maybe give it another try.

t_ch: 跟丢目标了，行动失败…再尝试一次吧。
t_en: You lost track of the target... maybe give it another try.

t_ch: 您有一条跨星际通讯信息，请及时查收。
t_en: You have an interstellar message. Please check it promptly.

t_ch: 喔？唔……
t_en: Hmm? Whew...

t_ch: 怎么了，帕姆？
t_en: What's up, Pom-Pom?

t_ch: 列车刚刚收到了一条从罗浮仙舟发来的消息，从摘要来看，似乎会和接下来的行程计划有冲突。
t_en: Just got a message from the Xianzhou Luofu. Looks like it'll conflict with our original schedule.

t_ch: 许久不见了，星穹列车的朋友们，不知各位的开拓之旅是否顺利？
t_en: It's been a while, my friends on the Astral Express. How's your trailblazing expedition going?

t_ch: 近日罗浮仙舟即将举行庆礼「星天演武」仪典。诸位曾帮助罗浮弭平灾厄，是仙舟联盟的好朋友。在此景元代表神策府诚邀诸位莅临观礼，请诸位务必拨冗赏光。
t_en: Soon, the Xianzhou Luofu will be holding the Luminary Wardance. Those who have aided the Luofu in overcoming the crisis are cherished allies of the Xianzhou, thus, on behalf of the Seat of Divine Foresight, I'm extending an invitation to attend the ceremony. Your presence would be greatly appreciated.

t_ch: 还真是热闹啊，才从家族的「谐乐大典」中抽身，这么快就有了新的邀请。
t_en: Well, things are getting lively. We've barely recovered from The Family's Charmony Festival, and we're already being invited to another special event.

t_ch: 开拓，就是快乐。
t_en: Trailblazing is all about having fun.

t_ch: 这么一想，咱们「开拓之旅」就要变成了到处吃吃喝喝玩玩的「快落之旅」。
t_en: Why not think about it this way. Our trailblazing expeditions are turning into "blast expeditions," where we eat, drink, and play wherever we go.

t_ch: 快…落？
t_en: Blast... expedition?

t_ch: 是啊，吃吃喝喝玩玩，一路快速堕落的「快落」。
t_en: Yeah, wherever we go, we eat, play, and have a blast!

t_ch: 对「盛会」都有心理创伤了。
t_en: I'm still recovering from the trauma caused by the last "festivity."

t_ch: 咱也一样！
t_en: Yeah, me too!

t_ch: 还有还有，连赶两场「盛会」活动，是不是显得有些不务正业了？列车的正事该是恢复星图和航路数据，顺带做些严肃的科考工作吧？
t_en: But wait, won't it look unprofessional if we attend two ceremonies in a row? Aren't we supposed to be focused on restoring astral charts and doing some serious scientific exploration?

t_ch: 那就让三月留下维护列车？
t_en: So, we should leave March behind to take care of the Express?

t_ch: 我、我可没说不想去。我是很喜欢凑热闹的啦…只是希望下一个凑热闹的地方不要再冒出个什么星期五、星期六来跟我们作对。
t_en: H—Hey! That's not what I meant! I'm all for some fun... I just hope there won't be any surprise party crashers like Friday or Saturday.

t_ch: 罗浮仙舟才度过了危机，举办演武仪典也是在对外彰显自己和平安全的状态。
t_en: The Xianzhou Luofu has recently overcome a crisis. By holding the Wardance, they're demonstrating to everyone that they've returned to a state of peace and safety.

t_ch: 去匹诺康尼之前大家也是这么说的！「在『家族』的保护下不会有任何危险～」
t_en: But that's what everyone said before we went to Penacony! "You'll be totally safe under The Family's protection!"

t_ch: 倒也不必杯弓蛇影。演武仪典不似「谐乐大典」那样隐藏了诸多秘密。它只是为纪念帝弓与云骑对抗丰饶孽物，拯救仙舟的壮举而设的节庆罢了。
t_en: No need to worry. The Wardance is not like the Charmony Festival with all its hidden secrets. It's just a festival to honor the Reignbow Arbiter and the Cloud Knights who fought against the Abominations of Abundance and protected the Xianzhou ships.

t_ch: 典礼前后除去星槎巡航的演出外，不过是些比武斗剑的竞赛。和我们去看过的泰科铵机动球没什么区别。
t_en: Aside from starskiff performances, it's mostly martial contests, nothing too different from the Taikiyan Roboball contest we've seen before.

t_ch: 姬子，你觉得如何？按照列车目前的状况，我们接受了黑天鹅女士的提议，应该安排一次去翁法罗斯的「开拓之旅」，补充燃料。
t_en: What do you think, Himeko? Since we've accepted Miss Black Swan's proposal, we should probably head to Amphoreus for refueling.

t_ch: 时间倒也不算紧迫。这次「开拓之旅」情况特殊，在去往下一站前，列车也应做好补给、支援方面的准备。
t_en: Mm, there's certainly no rush. This trailblazing expedition is quite unique, and the Express needs to be fully stocked and prepared before moving on to the next stop.

t_ch: 正巧有黑塔女士牵线，我本打算在启程前为天才俱乐部#81阮•梅女士从「金伦加深域」带回一具古兽遗骸，做些人情交换。不过这么一算，至少需要几周时间才能返航。
t_en: With Madam Herta's help, I was planning to deliver some Leviathan fossils from Calenga Abyss to Ruan Mei, member #81 of the Genius Society. It could earn us some favors before we set off. However, it may take a few weeks.

t_ch: 喔，那就是…去不成罗浮啦。
t_en: Ah, so that means... we're not going to the Luofu.

t_ch: 小三月，维系关系，这就是成年人生活所必须支付的小小代价。既然罗浮仙舟发出了邀请，那么作为朋友，星穹列车也理当赴约。
t_en: Being an adult means maintaining relationships, whether we like it or not, March. Since we've been invited, it's only right for the Astral Express to attend the ceremony.

t_ch: 我打算这么安排：帕姆会将所有人先送往罗浮。之后，我和瓦尔特先生则继续前往完成与阮•梅的约定。{NICKNAME}、三月和丹恒三人则可以作为列车观礼的代表出席仪典。
t_en: So, here's the plan: Pom-Pom will take everyone to the Xianzhou Luofu. Mr. Yang and I will meet up with Ruan Mei and fulfill our promise. Meanwhile, {NICKNAME}, March, and Dan Heng will represent the Express and attend the Wardance.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，丹恒，没问题吧？
t_en: What do you two think, {NICKNAME} and Dan Heng?

t_ch: 那分组就这么定了！
t_en: Sounds great. Let's split into groups!

t_ch: 看起来大家都没什么异议。
t_en: I don't hear any objections.

t_ch: 我要玩古兽化石！
t_en: I want to play with Leviathan fossils, too!

t_ch: 姬子姐和杨叔可是去正经搞科研呢！古兽化石哪有看演武斗剑好玩？
t_en: Himeko and Mr. Yang will be busy with serious research. Besides, fossils can't compete with martial contests when it comes to fun, right?

t_ch: 再说了，就属你在罗浮朋友多，你不去我都不知道该去哪玩！你得带路啊。
t_en: Plus, you're the only one with so many friends on the Luofu. I'll be lost if you don't come along! You've got to lead the way.

t_ch: 既然各位乘客已经达成一致了，那我们就准备跃迁前往仙舟「罗浮」了帕。
t_en: Now that everyone's on board with the plan, it's time to warp to the Xianzhou Luofu.

t_ch: 「金伦加深域」…她想从那儿找到什么呢？目前所有关于古兽的研究只是证明了我们对这种生命一无所知……
t_en: Calenga Abyss... what does she expect to find there? Current research on Leviathan merely proves how little we know about such life forms...

t_ch: 一无所知，这才是天才会出手的领域。科学要做的就是从无知的帷幕上挑破一个孔……
t_en: That's why geniuses are interested in that field. Science is all about uncovering the unknown...

t_ch: 两个成年人的交谈中充斥着复杂的术语，从「假定型天体生物化学」到「跨维度演化论」，车厢中充满了沉闷费解的空气。
t_en: The conversation between the two adults brims with complex jargon, from "Assumptive Astrobiological Chemistry" to "Trans-dimensional Evolutionary Theory," filling the carriage with an air of dense perplexity.

t_ch: 两位，一路顺风。
t_en: Have a safe trip, you two.

t_ch: 早点回来啊。
t_en: Come back soon.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，别太想我们喔。要是看到什么有趣的古兽化石，我会带上几块作为伴手礼送给你的。
t_en: Don't miss us too much, {NICKNAME}. If I stumble upon some cool Leviathan fossils, I'll bring a few back as souvenirs for you.

t_ch: 姬子姐真是懂人心啊。嘻嘻，在古兽化石和演武仪典之间，还是后者听起来比较有意思。
t_en: Himeko really knows how to convince people. Hehe, between Leviathan fossils and the Wardance, the latter definitely sounds more fun.

t_ch: 对了…丹恒，这次去罗浮，你会跟我们一块儿下车走走的吧？
t_en: By the way, Dan Heng... this time, you'll be taking a stroll with us on the Luofu, right?

t_ch: 放你和{NICKNAME}两个在罗浮上乱逛？我可不放心。
t_en: Just let you and {NICKNAME} wander around aimlessly on the Luofu by yourselves? I don't think so.

t_ch: 而且瓦尔特先生说的没错，建木灾异刚刚平息，无论是长生种还是短生种都对灾难心有余悸。景元选在此时举办演武仪典，显然是为了对外彰显罗浮的安定。
t_en: Plus, Mr. Yang is right. The Ambrosial Arbor Crisis just ended, and both the long-life and short-life species are still feeling uneasy. And that's why Jing Yuan wants to organize the Wardance, to show that the Xianzhou Luofu is stable and safe.

t_ch: 既然他发出邀请，于情于理，我都应该去看看老朋友。
t_en: Since he has extended an invitation, it's only right that I visit my old friend.

t_ch: 舒适的沙发，每次跃迁你都会坐在上面静静等候失重瞬间的来临。
t_en: The cozy sofa, your chosen sanctuary during each warp, where you calmly await the fleeting thrill of weightlessness.

t_ch: 星穹列车即将发车前往罗浮仙舟，请各位乘客坐稳扶好！
t_en: Buckle up, everyone! The Astral Express is about to depart for the Xianzhou Luofu!

t_ch: 跃迁结束后……
t_en: After the warp ends...

t_ch: 故地重游，本姑娘内心真是颇有一番感慨……
t_en: Coming back to this place brings back so many memories, you know...

t_ch: 下面请听三月七诗朗诵…
t_en: So, here's something for all of you, a poem by March 7th...

t_ch: 也没有到要为此情此景吟诗的地步啦！只是想到上一回抵达仙舟时的波折和惊悚……
t_en: Hey, I'm not actually gonna recite a poem! I was just thinking about all the twists and turns we went through when we first arrived on the Xianzhou...

t_ch: 什么感慨，说来听听？
t_en: What memories? Care to spill the details?

t_ch: 也不是什么了不得的诗情画意啦，只是想到上一回抵达仙舟时的波折和惊悚……
t_en: Well, it's nothing too poetic. I was just thinking about all the twists and turns we went through when we first arrived at the Xianzhou ship...

t_ch: 这一回咱们既没有被人半胁迫半诱骗，也不是为了追捕什么通缉犯，更不是从卸货码头登陆，如此一帆风顺……
t_en: This time, we're not being forced or enticed, or chasing after wanted criminals. And we didn't have to sneak in through the cargo dock. This trip has been incredibly smooth...

t_ch: 真是难得啊！
t_en: Quite unusual, I must say!

t_ch: 难得啊！
t_en: Definitely unusual!

t_ch: 难得啊。
t_en: I agree.

t_ch: 如此容易满足，我们好可怜啊…
t_en: We're so easily pleased. I actually feel sorry for us...

t_ch: 没办法，这就是无名客的宿命吧。
t_en: Such is the fate of us Nameless, I suppose.

t_ch: 求求你别乌鸦嘴了…
t_en: Oh, come on, don't jinx us...

t_ch: 我这是美好的祝愿，你这才叫乌鸦嘴吧！
t_en: Haha, I'm just hoping for the best! You're the one jinxing us!

t_ch: 星槎海有这么热闹吗？今天人真是格外多啊！都快听不清彼此说话了！
t_en: Is the Starskiff Haven always this crowded? There are so many people here! I can barely hear anything!

t_ch: 彦卿说，将军派他来接咱们，可他人在哪儿呢？
t_en: Yanqing said General Jing Yuan sent him to welcome us, but where is he?

t_ch: 我们去织机前面等他吧。那是码头上最显眼的地标。
t_en: Let's wait for him in front of the loom. It's the most prominent landmark on the dock.

t_ch: 三位，三位，请留步！
t_en: Hey, you guys! Hold on a moment!

t_ch: 那两个人是不是在叫我们啊？看打扮…像是匹诺康尼人？
t_en: Did they just call us? Look at their outfits... they're from Penacony, right?

t_ch: 不知你们几位是不是熟悉罗浮仙舟的情况？
t_en: Are you familiar with the Xianzhou Luofu?

t_ch: 略知一二。请问有什么事吗？
t_en: We know a little bit about it. What do you need?

t_ch: 我们俩来自匹诺康尼，也许你听说过这个地方？这次来到仙舟，是为了采集一些有趣的梦境素材。
t_en: We're from Penacony, maybe you've heard of it? We came to this ship to gather interesting materials for making dream bubbles.

t_ch: 巧了，我们刚从那儿离开。
t_en: We just left there. Talk about coincidence.

t_ch: 那就太好了，你们知道在这「罗浮」之上，有什么值得一去的景点吗？
t_en: That's great! Do you know any must-see attractions on the Luofu?

t_ch: 那你可问对人了！
t_en: You've come to the right people!

t_ch: 那是！我们仨可是仙舟通！
t_en: Exactly! We're Xianzhou experts!

t_ch: 这应该问本地人吧？
t_en: Why don't you ask the locals?

t_ch: 我们问了啊！但本地人都回答说：「罗浮的景点有啥好去的，都没意思！」
t_en: We did! But the locals were all like, "Come on, there's nothing worth seeing on the Luofu! It's so boring here!"

t_ch: 也可以理解。如果有人问我：「匹诺康尼都有什么值得去的景点呢？」我恐怕也会一时语塞。
t_en: Can't blame them. If someone asked me, "What's there to see on Penacony?" I'd probably struggle to answer too.

t_ch: 所以这种事还是要问其他游客啦！
t_en: That's why we're asking fellow tourists!

t_ch: 这段时间来的游客，多半会去参观演武仪典。我们几个就是为此而来的啊。
t_en: Most of the tourists around at the moment are here to attend the Wardance, and that's why we're here too.

t_ch: 我当然知道演武仪典的。但这仪典的擂台不是还没开放吗？
t_en: Yeah, we know about that ceremony, but isn't the fighting ring still closed?

t_ch: 我听说这一次的演武擂台，是拿罗浮退役的巨型斗舰改装而成的。
t_en: I've heard the ring was actually converted from a huge decommissioned Luofu fighter jet.

t_ch: 比一般的星槎还大？
t_en: So, it's even larger than a regular starskiff?

t_ch: 那还用说！这可是巨型斗舰，肯定比民用的星槎大多了！
t_en: No doubt about it! It's a massive fighter jet! It's got to be larger than a civilian starskiff!

t_ch: 比「晖长石号」还大？
t_en: So, it's even bigger than the Radiant Feldspar?

t_ch: 那多半是「晖长石号」更大吧！
t_en: The Radiant Feldspar is bigger, I guess!

t_ch: 不过咱现在只能等到几周后演武仪典开幕，才有机会登舰了。在此之前，我们俩也不能耽误工作——所以才问你们，有什么比较值得一去的景点？
t_en: But for now, all we can do is wait until the Wardance starts in a few weeks before we can board it. We've still got work to do, so we can't just sit around waiting for it to start, that's why we're asking you about some must-see attractions.

t_ch: 我们希望能找到那种，在匹诺康尼的日常梦境中难得一见的特殊体验。我们的客户最喜欢这种梦泡了。
t_en: We're looking for unique experiences that you won't find on Penacony. Our clients love these kinds of dream bubbles the most.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，这里你最熟，快给他们出出主意吧～
t_en: You're the expert here, {NICKNAME}. Give them some suggestions!

t_ch: 去金人巷吧。
t_en: Why not visit Aurum Alley?

t_ch: 这是个不错的主意。金人巷的人间烟火气，在「盛会之星」上难得一见。
t_en: Hmm, good idea. The everyday vibe of the Aurum Alley is a rare find on the Planet of Festivities.

t_ch: 人间烟火气啊…确实，现在我们这些客户啊，尝遍了惊险刺激的梦境。也许反而是这类接地气的内容能让他们找回感动。
t_en: Everyday vibe... Yeah, our clients have had their fair share of adrenaline-pumping Dreamscapes. Maybe something down-to-earth would touch their hearts.

t_ch: 感谢三位的指点，我们这就去金人巷采风。也许过两天，我们会在那里再度相遇。
t_en: Thanks for the suggestion. We'll head to the Aurum Alley to gather materials. Maybe we'll run into each other there in a couple of days.

t_ch: 去绥园吧。
t_en: Why not visit Fyxestroll Garden?

t_ch: 对对对，去绥园！那里可是罗浮著名的灵异圣地，到处都飘着些调皮的小鬼火，可有意思了！
t_en: Oh, yeah! Fyxestroll Garden! It's famous for supernatural stuff, with those mischievous heliobi floating around! It's pretty fascinating!

t_ch: 灵异？我不太喜欢恐怖题材…不过，我们的客户一定会喜欢。
t_en: Supernatural? I don't really like scary things... but our clients will love it.

t_ch: 听起来确实有点意思。那我们就去你们说的「绥园」啦～之后咱也许能在绥园再见！
t_en: It does sound interesting. Let's go check out Fyxestroll Garden! Maybe we'll bump into each other there!

t_ch: 去鳞渊境吧。
t_en: Why not visit Scalegorge Waterscape?

t_ch: 这倒是个好主意。梦境中尽是些恒常不变的人造景观，但这鳞渊境却是「自然」侵蚀「人造」而缔造的独特风景。
t_en: That's indeed a good idea. While the Dreamscape on Penacony is all artificial, Scalegorge Waterscape is a celebration of nature's resurgence. It has some remarkable scenery.

t_ch: 这个好，我最喜欢自然风光了。那我们就先去鳞渊境吧。
t_en: Awesome. I love being out there in nature. Let's go to Scalegorge Waterscape first.

t_ch: 我有点担心，自然风光相关的梦泡会不会有些过时…不过无妨，我们去看看吧。
t_en: I'm a bit worried that nature-themed dream bubbles might be outdated... but hey, let's go check it out anyway.

t_ch: 再见，也许这几天我们会在鳞渊境再相遇。
t_en: See you later. Maybe we'll run into each other there in a few days.

t_ch: 去爬上建木之巅吧！
t_en: Why not climb to the top of the Ambrosial Arbor?

t_ch: 喂！不要乱给别人出这种会被抓起来的馊主意啦！
t_en: Hey! That's a terrible idea! You'll get them arrested!

t_ch: 哈哈…你真有意思。还是给我们提供些更靠谱的建议吧。
t_en: Haha... you're just joking, right? How about giving us some more practical suggestions?

t_ch: …等一下。这个主意未必是个馊主意。富贵险中求啊！走吧，我们去爬树！
t_en: ...Wait a sec. Maybe it's not such a terrible idea after all? No risk, no reward! Come on, let's go climb that tree!

t_ch: 你跑慢点，等我一下。
t_en: Slow down a bit. Wait for me.

t_ch: 喂！别走的那么快！喂…至少出了事别把我们供出来啊！唉，他们不会惹出什么事端吧…真令人不安。
t_en: Hey, wait! Just think about what you're doing! And... don't mention our names if something goes wrong! Ugh, I hope they don't get into trouble... This is nerve-wracking.

t_ch: 你们看，彦卿似乎已经到了。
t_en: Look, Yanqing's here.

t_ch: 真的诶！我们赶紧去找他汇合吧！
t_en: Really! Let's go catch up with him!

t_ch: 各位，好久不见了啊！
t_en: Hey, everyone! It's been a while!

t_ch: 虽然感觉距离咱们上次离开也没过多久，但彦卿你是不是……
t_en: Doesn't feel like it's been that long since we last saw you, but Yanqing, are you...?

t_ch: 怎么啦，三月小姐？
t_en: What's up, Miss March?

t_ch: 都说只要一不留神，小娃娃会突然长成熟人也认不出的样子来。彦卿，你是不是…长高了一点儿？
t_en: They say kids grow up really fast. Yanqing, are you a little bit taller than before?

t_ch: 我们才离开了几个月时间。
t_en: We've only been away for a few months.

t_ch: 好久不见，彦卿！
t_en: Long time no see, Yanqing!

t_ch: 好久不见！
t_en: Long time no see!

t_ch: 好久不见。
t_en: It's been a while.

t_ch: 哎，丹恒先生也来了啊。
t_en: Oh, you've come along, too, Mr. Dan Heng.

t_ch: 让各位久等了。演武仪典在即，星槎海又是罗浮的交通枢纽，我光是从人群中找人就花了不少时间。
t_en: Sorry for the delay. With the upcoming Wardance, and the Starskiff Haven being so busy, things are a little hectic.

t_ch: 确实，你长高了！
t_en: Yeah, you're definitely taller!

t_ch: 真的吗？将军一直还担心我个子长不高呢。我一直劝他安心。
t_en: Really? The General is always worried I won't grow much taller, but I keep telling him not to worry about it.

t_ch: 让各位久等了。演武仪典在即，星槎海又是罗浮的交通枢纽，我光是从人群中找人就花了不少时间。
t_en: Sorry for the delay. With the upcoming Wardance, and the Starskiff Haven being so busy, things are a little hectic.

t_ch: （警觉地捏一捏彦卿的脸颊）
t_en: (Cautiously pinch Yanqing's cheek)

t_ch: 唉？{NICKNAME}，你干嘛？
t_en: Huh? What are you doing, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 抱歉，连续几站的冒险让我养成了警惕的习惯，每个地方第一个出现迎接我们的人，都要当心。
t_en: Sorry, but after our previous adventures, I've become suspicious of whoever greets us first.

t_ch: 对我也这么警惕？
t_en: Do you have to be suspicious around me, too?

t_ch: 要知道，上一次来仙舟，第一个迎接我们的可是……
t_en: You know, the last time we came to the Xianzhou, the first person who greeted us was...

t_ch: 彦卿明白了，警惕点好。
t_en: I get it. Better safe than sorry.

t_ch: 说真的，这是我第一次在罗浮上见到这么热闹的景象。
t_en: Seriously, I've never seen the Luofu so lively before.

t_ch: 我原本还有些担心，建木危机刚刚过去，举行演武仪典是否有些仓促。但看看现在的星槎海，将军选择的时机是恰当的。
t_en: I was a bit worried that holding the Wardance right after the Ambrosial Arbor crisis might be too soon, but seeing the bustling Starskiff Haven, I understand why General Jing Yuan chose this timing.

t_ch: 嗯，这些往来行人里不仅有其他洞天的居民，还有像三位这样远道而来的客人。
t_en: Yep, there's people from other delves and travelers like you three who've come from afar.

t_ch: 演武仪典召开在即，星槎海的客流吞吐连上了几个台阶。云骑的安全工作也比过去忙上了好几倍。
t_en: With the Wardance coming up, there's a huge number of visitors pouring into the Starskiff Haven. The Cloud Knights are working hard to keep the security tight.

t_ch: 将军说了，想要让罗浮从灾后重整旗鼓，恰恰需要这场演武仪典。
t_en: The General said this ceremony would help the Xianzhou Luofu recover from the crisis.

t_ch: 彰显武德、安稳民心、提振士气，还能借仪典之机邀请友邦客人来访，促成贸易与和平。
t_en: It's a way to showcase our martial spirit, reassure people, boost morale, and attract visitors from other planets to promote trade and peace.

t_ch: 说起来，联盟对本次仪典还挺重视的，「朱明」和「曜青」两座仙舟都派遣使者来贺了！
t_en: By the way, the Xianzhou Alliance places great importance on this ceremony, too. The Xianzhou ships the Zhuming and the Yaoqing have both sent messengers to offer their blessings!

t_ch: 朱明仙舟？
t_en: The Xianzhou Zhuming?

t_ch: 是啊，不知{NICKNAME}是否听过它「千星锻炉」的名头，云骑军的军器技术十之五六都由它提供。
t_en: Yeah, I wonder if you've heard of it, {NICKNAME}. It's known as the "Starforge" and is responsible for providing more than half of the Cloud Knights' armaments.

t_ch: 朱明仙舟一直以能工巧匠著称，听说朱明的怀炎将军自己就是工匠中的翘楚。啊…要是能有幸得到他亲手锻造的宝剑，我怕是做梦都要笑醒了。
t_en: The Xianzhou Zhuming boasts skilled craftsmasters, and General Huaiyan is a top-notch craftsman himself. Ah... if only I could get a sword forged by him! I'd be on cloud nine.

t_ch: 曜青仙舟？
t_en: The Xianzhou Yaoqing?

t_ch: 嗯，曜青仙舟是联盟巡猎孽物的锋镝，常年在外征战四方。虽说同属联盟，但我对那里知道的也不算多。
t_en: Yeah, the Xianzhou Yaoqing is a major force in hunting down abominations and is always engaged in conflicts across the cosmos. Although it's a member of the Xianzhou Alliance, I don't know much about it.

t_ch: 只听天舶司的驭空大人说，曜青发来的战报上回回「大捷」，令人印象深刻。
t_en: But Madam Yukong from the Sky-Faring Commission mentioned that the Yaoqing always sends back reports of great victories, which is quite impressive.

t_ch: 听说曜青的飞霄将军年纪轻轻，却勇冠三军。云骑军里同僚们将她传得神乎其神，说她一身西瓜般的腱子肉，一拳下去便能轰碎十来头孽物…也不知是真是假。
t_en: I heard General Feixiao of the Yaoqing is a young and dauntless lady. My peers say that she's like a goddess of war, capable of crushing dozens of abominations with a single punch... I wonder if it's true.

t_ch: …还真想见识见识她的身手啊。
t_en: ...If only I could witness her prowess with my own eyes.

t_ch: 对了彦卿，下一步咱们该去哪？
t_en: By the way, Yanqing, where are we headed next?

t_ch: 啊，你瞧我光杵在这儿拉拉杂杂说了一大堆。将军唤我接各位去司辰宫小坐，他也很久没见各位了，想听你们聊聊列车的近况呢。
t_en: Ah, I'm sorry for talking your ear off. The General wants to catch up with you at the Palace of Astrum. He's been eager to hear about how the Express has been doing.

t_ch: 一谈到自己感兴趣的事物，这小大人就露出了孩子气的一面，嘿嘿。
t_en: Hehe, it's funny how he tries to act all mature, but whenever it comes to something he's interested in, you can really see his childish side.

t_ch: 要这么说来，你们俩还挺像的。
t_en: Well, you're pretty much the same in that regard.

t_ch: 确实。
t_en: I agree.

t_ch: 他真可爱。
t_en: Yeah, he's adorable.

t_ch: 确实。
t_en: I agree.

t_ch: （激烈的嘶吼）
t_en: (frantic roars)

t_ch: 各单位迅速集结！注意保护人群。
t_en: All units, assemble quickly! Get ready to protect the crowd!

t_ch: 刚刚还说街上的安全工作不容闪失，怎么就有事情发生了。抱歉各位，我得先去探探情况。
t_en: I just mentioned security, and now all of a sudden something's gone wrong. Excuse me, I need to go check out the situation.

t_ch: 真是怕什么来什么。
t_en: It's like whatever you fear will happen, happens.

t_ch: 你和三月一样言出法随。
t_en: You're just as jinxed as March.

t_ch: 说什么呢！
t_en: Hey, what's that supposed to mean!?

t_ch: 我们和你一起去。
t_en: We'll go with you.

t_ch: 事出突然，要耽搁几位一会儿工夫了。容我探问一下刚才到底是什么情况。
t_en: I'm sorry, but I'm afraid we'll have to put our plans on hold for now. I need to find out what's going on.

t_ch: 谁是负责这支队伍的长官？我需要个解释。
t_en: Who's in charge here? I need some answers.

t_ch: 是我…抱歉，我们一时看守失察，都是我的责任。
t_en: It's my fault... we let our guard down for a moment. I take full responsibility.

t_ch: 来，说说吧，演武仪典召开在即，罗浮的一切以安全稳定为上。为什么在星槎海会出现步离囚犯？
t_en: With the Wardance approaching, safety should be a top priority. Now, tell me, how did borisin prisoners end up in Starskiff Haven?

t_ch: 按照流程，危险重犯应当收押在星槎中，全程不落地送往幽囚狱。是谁允许押解犯人的船停靠在客运码头的！
t_en: According to the protocol, borisin prisoners should be held on a starskiff and taken directly to The Shackling Prison under strict supervision without ever touching the ground. Who allowed a prisoner transport ship to dock at the passenger terminal?

t_ch: 怪不得这位队长，是朱明使节舰太过热心了。
t_en: Please don't blame this captain. This incident involves the Zhuming's diplomatic vessel.

t_ch: 你又是谁？
t_en: Who are you?

t_ch: 在下路君，巡防卫队的值守武官。多谢彦卿骁卫出手相助。事起仓促，绝非这位队长的过错。
t_en: I'm Lujun, an officer of the patrol defense squad. Thank you for your help, Lieutenant Yanqing. The situation unfolded rapidly, and it shouldn't be held against the captain.

t_ch: 公司途径罗浮中转的运输船在即将抵达之际遭到了步离人的袭击。朱明的使节舰出手相助，击溃了这股步离劫匪并收押在船舱里。
t_en: Here's what happened. An IPC transport ship was attacked by the borisin just before arriving, and the Zhuming's diplomatic vessel came to the rescue. They fought off the borisin pirates and imprisoned them on their ship.

t_ch: 在罗浮航行的宙域，公司的舰船，被步离人打劫，而朱明的使节救了他们？这案情听起来真复杂……
t_en: So, an IPC ship was attacked by the borisin near the Luofu, and the Zhuming envoys saved them? This sounds complicated...

t_ch: 属下也是百思不得其解。朱明的使节舰按规矩，停靠在客船码头，将这群犯人移交罗浮这边。
t_en: Honestly, it gives me a headache too. The Zhuming diplomatic ship, adhering to standard procedure, docked at the passenger terminal to hand these criminals over to the Luofu.

t_ch: 您也知道，这些日子来，数不清的外客涌入罗浮，星槎航道压力陡增。还没等来押解的星槎与航道管制，步离人狂徒便狗急跳墙了。于是有了方才您看到的一幕。
t_en: You know, with all the outsiders flooding onto the Luofu, the starskiff lanes are under immense pressure. The borisin desperadoes decided to put up a fight before the prisoner transport starskiff could get there, and that's what you just witnessed.

t_ch: 我们会尽快将这些人犯送往幽囚狱的。
t_en: We'll make sure these prisoners are sent to The Shackling Prison as soon as possible.

t_ch: 如此看来，确实有事急从权之处。我会将此事呈报给地衡司的治安厅，让他们配合善后。
t_en: I see, it's an unusual situation indeed. I'll report it to the Security Department of the Realm-Keeping Commission and ask for their cooperation in handling the aftermath.

t_ch: （也许该听听当事人打算如何善后处理，也方便神策府了解情况，）
t_en: (Maybe I should gather more details from others so that the Seat of Divine Foresight can have a better understanding of the situation.)

t_ch: 我和弟兄们会尽快将这群劫匪送进幽囚狱，善后工作也在进行，彦卿骁卫请勿担心。
t_en: We'll see to it that these criminals are sent to The Shackling Prison as soon as possible. The cleanup operation is already underway, Lieutenant Yanqing, so there's no need to worry about anything.

t_ch: 有劳了。你是…巡防卫队的武官？看起来似乎面生的很……
t_en: Thanks. By the way, you're... an officer of the patrol defense squad, right? I don't remember meeting you before...

t_ch: 哪儿的话，将军侍卫又怎么会记得我这样的小小角色。在下刚刚随出征部队返抵罗浮，因为仪典召开前的安全工作，被临时调派至此。
t_en: Well, I'm just a junior officer. I recently returned to the Luofu from the front lines and was temporarily assigned here for security duty before the ceremony.

t_ch: 仙舟的朋友，你们虽然救了我们，我们是很感激。但连我们带货物也一块扣押，这是不是就有些不像话了？
t_en: While we appreciate your rescue, my Xianzhou friends, don't you think it's a bit too much to detain us and our cargo?

t_ch: 我们收到命令，在查清袭击事件前因后果之前，必须暂时扣押贵方的货物进行检查。
t_en: Sorry, but we've been ordered to detain you and your cargo for inspection until we figure out the source of the attack.

t_ch: 只要事后各方手续勘验完成，我们会连人带货一起放行的。
t_en: Once we're done with the formalities, we'll let you and your cargo go.

t_ch: 这批货物压根就不是运往罗浮的，还是公司的专利科技，你们凭什么「检查」啊？
t_en: But this shipment isn't even meant for the Luofu! And it's IPC's patented technology. Who do you think you are to conduct an "inspection?"

t_ch: 按照流程，任何抵达罗浮的货物都必须经过检查。
t_en: According to the protocol, all cargo arriving on the Luofu must go through inspection.

t_ch: 我们也不算入港啊！我们是遭遇了步离人的袭击，才不得不来罗浮的港口避难啊！
t_en: But we didn't officially enter your dock at all! We just sought refuge in your dock because we were attacked by the borisin!

t_ch: 看起来，他们会争论很久。像这样吵不出个结果的纠纷，咱们还是不要卷入为妙。
t_en: Looks like this argument could go on forever. Let's not get involved in a heated dispute that won't lead to a resolution.

t_ch: 云骑军安抚了人群，随即散去，长街再度恢复了平静。
t_en: The Cloud Knights calm the crowd, then dispersed, and the street was restored to its peaceful state.

t_ch: 彦卿，你真是越来越有大人的模样了。
t_en: You look much maturer now, Yanqing.

t_ch: 三月小姐，你就别损我啦。演武仪典前仙舟的安全就像一根紧绷的弦，看似平静，但只要稍稍一拨就有余波动荡不休。
t_en: Please don't tease me, Miss March. The situation on the Xianzhou before the Wardance is like a calm lake that can be disturbed by even the smallest pebble — capable of generating far-reaching ripples with even the slightest disturbance.

t_ch: 咱们刚才收拾的那些…呃，那些家伙到底是什么来头啊？
t_en: What are those people... I mean, those "monsters" we just dealt with?

t_ch: 是狗头人吧！
t_en: I know! They're Kobolds, right?

t_ch: 这些狼首怪物，唤作步离人，是与仙舟长久为敌的丰饶孽物。
t_en: Those werewolf monsters are known as "borisin." They are Abominations of Abundance, and we've been fighting them for a very long time.

t_ch: 你叫他们步离人？
t_en: Did you call them "the borisin"?

t_ch: 没错。这些狼首怪物，唤作步离人，是与仙舟长久为敌的丰饶孽物。
t_en: Yeah, those werewolf monsters are known as "borisin." They are Abominations of Abundance, and we've been fighting them for a very long time.

t_ch: 长久以来，步离人势力众多，血腥掠夺和奴役着众多世界，其祸害比起寰宇虫灾也不遑多让。三十年前，联盟还曾和他们大战一场。
t_en: The borisin have been a powerful force for a long time, plundering and enslaving many worlds. The threat they pose is just as terrible as the Swarm Disaster, and the Alliance even had a fierce war with them three decades ago.

t_ch: 这些年里，他们的活动渐渐不再像往日那般猖獗了。谁能想到……
t_en: Their presence has faded over the years, but who would have thought...

t_ch: 按刚才那个武官所说，他们是在靠近罗浮的宙域袭击了公司的舰船？如此明目张胆的袭击，真是古怪。
t_en: According to that officer, they attacked an IPC ship near the Xianzhou Luofu. Such a brazen attack seems quite unusual to me.

t_ch: 我也觉得奇怪，看起来公司和他们结仇不小啊。
t_en: Yeah, that's what I find strange, too. It seems like the IPC and the borisin have some serious grudges.

t_ch: 闲话不提，该办正事了，将军要我接各位去司辰宫。
t_en: Well, enough with the chitchat. The general wants me to take you to the Palace of Astrum.

t_ch: 彦卿本想再和你们几位多聊聊天，唉，但有些事时时刻刻都让人放心不下……
t_en: I'd love to chitchat a little longer, but there are some things that can't be left unchecked...

t_ch: 「放心不下」？莫非是追捕步离人…需要咱们帮忙吗？
t_en: Hmm? Is it a serious matter? Maybe you'll... need our help in hunting down the borisin?

t_ch: 不用不用，一点小事罢了。
t_en: Thank you, but it's no big deal.

t_ch: 那个…刚刚出现的那个姑娘…把我的剑顺走了。我打算去地衡司报个失物案，看看能不能找回……
t_en: By the way... that young lady who just appeared... she took my sword. I'm thinking of filing a lost property report at the Realm-Keeping Commission to see if I can get it back...

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈，人家应该也不是故意的啦！
t_en: Hahaha, I doubt she did it on purpose!

t_ch: 云骑出了事也要报警吗？
t_en: So when Cloud Knights get into trouble, they have to call the emergency services too?

t_ch: 也许人家就没走远，不再找找吗?
t_en: Perhaps she's around here somewhere? Shouldn't we take a look?

t_ch: 报案吧，场面肯定很精彩…
t_en: You go file the report, and I go fetch the popcorn. It's gonna be quite the show...

t_ch: 求你别说了，是我一时失察。
t_en: Don't remind me. I just zoned out for a moment, that's all.

t_ch: 快走吧，咱们别让将军久等了。
t_en: Alright, let's not keep the general waiting.

t_ch: 别担心，有这样身手的女孩不多见，应该没那么难找。
t_en: Don't worry. There aren't many people out there with that kind of talent. It shouldn't be too hard to find her.

t_ch: 会带来麻烦的家伙还真不少啊……
t_en: There sure are a lot of troublemakers around...

t_ch: 将军，我将列车的客人接来了。不知道将军见客，彦卿来的不是时候。
t_en: General, I've brought our guests from the Express. I'm sorry for my bad timing. I didn't know you were meeting a guest, General.

t_ch: 无妨，你来得正是时候。星穹列车的诸位，好久不见！
t_en: Don't worry. You're just in time. It's been a while, my friends from the Astral Express!

t_ch: 将军，一切可好？
t_en: How's everything going, General?

t_ch: 有朋远来，自然一切都好。
t_en: All is well when you have friends coming from afar.

t_ch: 我在匹诺康尼梦到你了。
t_en: I dreamed about you on Penacony.

t_ch: 喔？我入故人梦，明我长相忆。能在诸位的美梦之中有一席之地，荣幸之至。
t_en: Oh? Well It's an honor to be in your dreams, my friend.

t_ch: 想我们了吗？
t_en: Did you miss us?

t_ch: 想，怎么能不想呢？数人世相逢，百年欢笑，能得几回又？
t_en: Sure, how couldn't I have? It's not often we find such moments to reunite and enjoy one another's presence.

t_ch: 丹恒，{NICKNAME}到底什么时候和将军混得这么熟了？两人说起话来谈笑风生的。
t_en: Since when did {NICKNAME} and General Jing Yuan become such good buddies, Dan Heng? They seem to be having a great time.

t_ch: 在我们不知道的时候，一定发生了不少事。最好是这样……
t_en: A lot must have happened without our knowledge. At least I hope that's the case...

t_ch: 容我向诸位引介，这位是仙舟「朱明」的天将，烛渊将军，怀炎。
t_en: Allow me to introduce you to General Huaiyan. He's the Arbiter-General of the Xianzhou Zhuming, known as the Flaming Heart.

t_ch: 哈哈。不必如此正式，老朽此行便衣简从，与来观礼的游客并无区别。
t_en: Hehe, no need to be so formal. I'm just a tourist here, no different from other tourists who've come to attend the ceremony.

t_ch: 炎老不仅是帝弓的将军，亦是工造司的百冶；戎马倥偬之外，更擅长百般工巧。如此人瑞，在天将中亦是独一无二。
t_en: Elder Huaiyan is not only one of the Arbiter-Generals but also the Furnace Master of the Artisanship Commission. Besides his martial skills, he excels in forging various weapons. Such talents are unique, even among the Arbiter-Generals.

t_ch: 将军也好、百冶也罢，都不过是应时加身的名头。老朽早已卸任数次，只是如今局势变化，元帅再度征召，我不得不走马上任。
t_en: Be it Arbiter-General or Furnace Master, these are merely titles given to me long ago. I've retired several times already, but with the current change in circumstances, the Marshal has called me back to duty, and I had no choice but to answer the call.

t_ch: 说到底也怪我，活得实在是太久了些，难免遭人非议。
t_en: Well, in the end, I'm to blame. Living such a long life naturally brings its share of disapproval.

t_ch: 拜见怀炎将军。
t_en: It's an honor to meet you, General Huaiyan.

t_ch: 拜见怀炎将军……
t_en: It's my honor to meet you, General Huaiyan...

t_ch: 见过老将军。
t_en: Glad to meet you, General.

t_ch: 诸位不必客气。今日我与你们一样，是罗浮的客人。
t_en: No need to be all formal. Today I'm just a guest on the Luofu, the same as all of you.

t_ch: 景元，这三位就是你之前提到的，助你弭平建木灾异的救星咯？
t_en: So, these three are the ones you mentioned, Jing Yuan? The heroes who helped you with the Ambrosial Arbor Crisis?

t_ch: 正是。丹恒，三月七，还有{NICKNAME}…若无这几位朋友力挽狂澜，罗浮怕不能轻松渡过此劫。
t_en: Indeed. There's Dan Heng, March 7th, and {NICKNAME}... Without their help, I'm afraid the Luofu might not have easily overcome this crisis.

t_ch: 饮月君的后世重回罗浮观礼演武仪典，若有机会，老朽想同你喝上一杯。
t_en: So, the Imbibitor Lunae's reincarnation has returned to the Luofu and will attend the Wardance. I'd love to have a drink with you should the chance presents itself.

t_ch: 丹恒随时奉陪。
t_en: You're more than welcome, General Huaiyan.

t_ch: 那旁边这位小朋友是……？
t_en: And this young friend is...?

t_ch: 我的弟子，彦卿。只因年纪尚浅，晚辈让他待在身边充任侍卫，希望能让他多受历练。这次演武仪典，他会代表罗浮云骑守擂竞锋，接受四方挑战。
t_en: Yanqing, my apprentice. He remains by my side as my retainer due to his youth, which I hope will season him with experience. He will stand for the Luofu's Cloud Knights in the upcoming Wardance, ready to take on all kinds of challenges.

t_ch: 好、好、好！老朽今日能一次得见这么多青年俊彦，真是不枉此行。
t_en: Great, great! It's a real treat to see so many talented young people around here today.

t_ch: 哦对了，上了年纪便容易忘事…旁边这位是我的徒孙，云璃……
t_en: Oh, I almost forgot... This is my apprentice, Yunli...

t_ch: 是你！
t_en: It's you!

t_ch: 拜见怀炎将军。
t_en: It's an honor to meet you, General Huaiyan.

t_ch: 拜见怀炎将军。
t_en: It's an honor to meet you, General Huaiyan.

t_ch: 喔，是你啊。你好。
t_en: Oh, it's you. Hello.

t_ch: 嚯嚯，你们认得啊，看来不用介绍了！
t_en: Oh, you two already know each other? Guess we don't need any introductions then!

t_ch: 真是踏破铁鞋无觅处，我本以为要费上不少工夫才能找到这位姑娘。
t_en: What a coincidence! I was afraid I'd have trouble finding this girl.

t_ch: 喔？老朽的好奇心都被吊起来了…快说说，你们俩怎么就互相认得了？
t_en: Oh? Now you've piqued my curiosity... Tell me, how did you two become acquainted?

t_ch: 这位姑娘在星槎海出手帮助镇伏逃跑的步离人囚犯，彦卿先在此谢过。但你离开时…也顺手带走了我的飞剑。
t_en: She helped me capture the escaped borisin prisoners at the Starskiff Haven. Allow me to express my gratitude for you, but when you left... you took my Flying Sword with you.

t_ch: 飞剑？…喔，我还在想怎么行囊里凭空多出一把短剑，原来是你的。
t_en: Your Flying Sword?... Oh, so that's why I found a dagger in my bag. Turns out it's yours.

t_ch: 正是。既然有缘再会，希望你能……
t_en: Yes, it is. Now that we've met again, I hope...

t_ch: 这恐怕不好。
t_en: Nope, that won't do.

t_ch: 不好？
t_en: Won't do?

t_ch: 你是想要回这把剑吧？可以，但不能是以这种方式。我们朱明的规矩，在战场上失去的剑，要在战场上拿回来。
t_en: You want your sword back, right? Well, you can't just take it back. On the Zhuming, when you lose your sword on the battlefield, you have to reclaim it on the battlefield.

t_ch: 这把小剑便是如此。它飞在空中，本该射向那名逃窜的步离人。可惜剑主心性纷扰，反倒让它像只折翼的飞鸢，失了准头。
t_en: As for this little sword, it was supposed to strike that escaped borisin prisoner, but unfortunately its owner's agitated state caused it to fly off like a kite with a broken string, and it missed its target.

t_ch: 况且，若不是我伸手接过这剑，助它命中目标，那步离人早已逃之夭夭了。
t_en: By the way, if I hadn't caught it and helped it hit its mark, that borisin prisoner would have gotten away.

t_ch: 云璃姑娘，你二话不说带走了我的剑，怎么还能若无其事地拒绝物归原主？
t_en: Hold on a sec, Yunli. You took my sword without even asking, and now you're refusing to give it back?

t_ch: …罗浮剑士，不过如此。
t_en: ...Heh, so much for Luofu swordmasters.

t_ch: 你说什么？
t_en: What did you just say?

t_ch: 你若能大大方方、堂堂正正地从我手上夺回自己的剑，云璃心服口服。可你没有，你竟想着大事化小，当着大庭广众随随便便让我还剑……
t_en: If you just stepped up and took your sword back from me fair and square, I would have totally respected you. But nope, you tried to play it down, expecting me to just hand it back to you like it's nothing, in front of everyone...

t_ch: 恕我直言，你对助我等杀敌护身的剑器毫无尊重，配不上这柄剑。
t_en: With all due respect, you don't honor your sword, so you don't deserve it.

t_ch: 姑娘，难道没人教过你「不告而取是为贼」吗？如果非要用剑说话…可以，就咱们俩，现在一对一……
t_en: Hasn't anyone told you that taking without asking is stealing? If you want to settle this with swords... Fine, let's have a one-on-one duel right now...

t_ch: 彦卿。
t_en: Yanqing.

t_ch: 嗯，这才对。你可得小心了，我不像步离人那般好对付。
t_en: Well, that's more like it. Just be careful, because I'm not as easy to handle as the borisin.

t_ch: 你也住嘴吧，赶快向彦卿弟弟赔罪！
t_en: You too. Be quiet and apologize to Yanqing!

t_ch: 爷爷，你到底站哪头的？
t_en: Hey, whose side are you on, Grandpa?

t_ch: 爷爷…呃，爷爷哪头都不站！
t_en: I... um... I don't take sides!

t_ch: 小小误会，「赔罪」言重了。耳闻朱明仙舟剑术与匠艺位于巅峰者有「焰轮八叶」的美名传世，今日见到名列其间的云璃小姐…确有火一般的风姿心性。
t_en: It's a small misunderstanding, and an apology would be too much. I've heard about the Zhuming's incredible swordplay and craftsmanship, most notably the legendary Flamewheel Octet. Seeing Miss Yunli, who is among those ranks today... Well I must say, she definitely has that fiery edge.

t_ch: …什么美名。总有好事之人热爱编撰虚名，为云骑乱立山头。云璃只是个孩子，面薄怕生，行事无礼之处还望各位见谅。
t_en: ...Such grandiose names. Some folks love to spin these fancy titles, trying to set the Cloud Knights apart. Yunli is still just a young girl, a bit awkward and hot-tempered. So please forgive her if she's being rude.

t_ch: 诸位，我和炎老还有些事要商量。眼下让彦卿招待几位客人与云璃小姐先去客栈入住。
t_en: Well, everyone, Elder Huaiyan and I have some business to discuss. For now, Yanqing, why don't you entertain our guests and take Miss Yunli to the inn?

t_ch: 之后我会另选时机与各位畅谈。好好答谢星穹列车当初救助罗浮于水火之中的恩情。
t_en: I'll find another chance to talk with you all. I'd like to express my gratitude to the Astral Express for helping the Luofu during the crisis.

t_ch: 真是客气，您都谢了好多回了。
t_en: That's so kind of you. I mean you've already thanked us so many times.

t_ch: 怪老朽来得不是时候，三位客人见谅。
t_en: Please forgive me for coming at an inconvenient time.

t_ch: 怀炎将军言重了。
t_en: You needn't apologize, General Huaiyan.

t_ch: 丫头，去吧，借这个机会好好和彦卿解开误会。
t_en: Alright, Yunli. Take this opportunity to clear things up with Yanqing.

t_ch: 是是是，冤家宜解不宜结。不过，我打算先不去客栈…我要见见灵砂姐姐。她刚到罗浮，正需要人帮忙安顿呢。
t_en: Yeah, yeah, it's better to make friends than enemies, but I won't be heading to the inn just yet... I want to visit Lingsha. She just arrived in the Luofu and could use some help settling in.

t_ch: 彦卿，安顿了客人后，替我再跑一趟工造司。我听闻公司舰船遇袭被扣押一事。青镞送来了消息，说公司的人正在抗议，想取回船上的货物。
t_en: Yanqing, once you've helped our guests get settled, go to the Artisanship Commission for me. I've heard about the attack and the detainment of the IPC ship. Qingzu sent word that the IPC members are protesting and wish to have their cargo back.

t_ch: 你代表我去安抚他们一下吧，不可太强硬，只要表达罗浮仙舟无意侵犯他们的权利就是。
t_en: See if you can calm them down. Don't get aggressive. Just make it clear that the Xianzhou Luofu has no intention of violating their rights.

t_ch: 彦卿领命。
t_en: I'm on it.

t_ch: 这一趟出行没白来。和朱明一比，罗浮真是热闹多了。
t_en: This trip is totally worth it. Compared to the Zhuming, the Luofu is so much livelier.

t_ch: 可惜论剑艺，罗浮的剑士似乎不太行……
t_en: But it's a shame the Luofu swordmasters don't seem that great...

t_ch: 你好，云璃小姐。
t_en: Glad to meet you, Miss Yunli.

t_ch: 你好，你是来挑战演武仪典的选手？
t_en: Glad to meet you, too. Are you here to participate in the Wardance?

t_ch: 只是来观礼的游客。
t_en: I'm just a tourist attending the ceremony.

t_ch: 原来不是选手么？我明明瞧你也是个练家子。
t_en: So, you're not a participant? You look like you know your way around a fight.

t_ch: 不过没关系，你也可以报名参加，云骑所设的擂台向来是不禁外人挑战的。
t_en: Well, you can still sign up. The Cloud Knights' ring is always open to outsiders.

t_ch: 演武仪典外人也能参加？
t_en: Can outsiders participate in the Wardance as well?

t_ch: 那是自然。为了彰显武德，云骑所设的擂台向来是不禁外人挑战的。
t_en: Of course. To honor martial arts, the Cloud Knights' ring is always open to outsiders.

t_ch: 若是能胜过仙舟所选的守擂人，不仅能赢得赏金和奖品，还能扬名立万。
t_en: If you can defeat the ringmaster selected by the Xianzhou, you'll not only win rewards and prizes but also be making a name for yourself.

t_ch: 怎么样，你心动了？
t_en: Sounds tempting, doesn't it?

t_ch: 将军说的「焰轮八叶」是？
t_en: What's the "Flamewheel Octet" the general spoke of?

t_ch: 那是外人对我那些在朱明云骑军和工造司里的师兄师姐们的称呼。
t_en: That's how outsiders refer to my peers in the Zhuming Cloud Knights and the Artisanship Commission.

t_ch: 我和这些人都拜在爷爷的名下学习匠艺和剑术。所以外人才会有如此称呼吧？
t_en: We all train under Grandpa's guidance, learning the art of craftsmanship and swordplay. That's how we got that title, I guess.

t_ch: 你可别在爷爷面前提这个。爷爷说啦，浮名伤人，徒然惹来挑战和争端。朱明剑士只以全军克捷为荣，不以一己胜利为喜。
t_en: Just don't mention it in front of Grandpa. He always says that empty titles bring pointless challenges and conflicts. We Zhuming swordmasters pride ourselves on the success of the group over the individual.

t_ch: 你不和我们一起走吗？
t_en: Aren't you coming with us?

t_ch: 我还有自己的事呢。再说了，彦卿小弟好像不太乐意。
t_en: I have some things to do. Besides, little Yanqing doesn't seem too happy about me tagging along.

t_ch: 他确实不该说你「偷」。
t_en: He shouldn't have said you "stole" his sword.

t_ch: 可你确实带走了他的剑…
t_en: But you did take his sword...

t_ch: 你说的不错。明明是凭本事「拿」走，说是抢也罢了，他怎么能将我和小偷小摸混为一谈？
t_en: You're right. I "took" it with my skills, so I guess you can say I'm a robber or something. How dare he imply I'm a thief. Totally different!

t_ch: 再说了，这柄剑如今有些畏惧它的主人。我倒要反问他，自诩惜剑如命，竟连自己掌中之剑如今是何状态都不认得。
t_en: Besides, the sword seems scared of its own master. I'd like to ask him – you claim to cherish your sword as your life, yet you don't even recognize the state it's in right now?

t_ch: 我原本也无意留下他的剑，想着趁这次机会把剑还他，不过眼下我改主意了。
t_en: I didn't intend to keep his sword. I was planning to take this chance to return it to him, but now I've changed my mind.

t_ch: 我见飞剑可怜，等会打算去做个养护。在那之前，除非他学会说「请，你好，谢谢，对不起」，我才会考虑把剑还给他。
t_en: I'll give that poor flying sword some proper maintenance. I won't consider returning the sword unless he learns how to say "please," "hello," "thank you," and "sorry."

t_ch: 现在就算了吧！
t_en: So he can forget about it for now!

t_ch: 你挠了挠头，忍不住为彦卿即将到来的苦难头皮发麻。就在你分神的片刻，少女已自顾自不知去向。
t_en: You scratch your head, feeling a tingling sensation on your scalp at the thought of the impending troubles Yanqing will face. While you're distracted, the young girl has already wandered off on her own, her whereabouts unknown.

t_ch: 丹恒啊，我还以为仙舟的将军个个都跟景元一样……
t_en: Hey, Dan Heng, I thought all the Xianzhou generals were like Jing Yuan...

t_ch: 但这位怀炎将军，却这么…呃，这么瘦瘦小小，一副迎风就倒的模样。这个干巴巴的老爷爷，真是帝弓天将吗？
t_en: But that General Huaiyan, he looks... so thin and scraggy, as if even a light breeze would knock him over. Is that old man really an Arbiter-General?

t_ch: 每座仙舟在联盟内各有所司，各有所长。帝弓的将军们也并非各个都擅于冲锋陷阵。你刚才听见景元的介绍了，怀炎先生曾是朱明的巧匠之首。
t_en: Each Xianzhou ship has its own division and specialty within the Alliance, not all of the Arbiter-Generals are good at leading the charge. You heard what Jing Yuan said, right? General Huaiyan was once the head of all the skilled craftsmen on the Zhuming.

t_ch: 一副深藏不露的模样。
t_en: There appears to be more than meets the eye.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你没感到奇怪吗？仙舟人会衰老吗？我在仙舟上可是很少见到老人，就算瞧见也是些异邦人。
t_en: Don't you find it strange, {NICKNAME}? Do Xianzhou people age? I rarely see any old people on the Xianzhou ship, and when I do, they're usually travelers.

t_ch: 但…这位怀炎将军看起来却像一个普普通通的老爷爷。他真的是仙舟将军吗？
t_en: But... General Huaiyan looks like an ordinary old grandpa. Is he really a Xianzhou general?

t_ch: 他为什么看起来如此衰老？
t_en: Why does he look so old?

t_ch: 你发现了盲点，好奇怪啊…仙舟人会衰老吗？我在仙舟上可是很少见到老人，就算瞧见也是些异邦人。
t_en: Yeah, you've noticed that too. It's really weird... Do the Xianzhou people age? I rarely see any old people on the Xianzhou ship, and when I do, they're usually travelers.

t_ch: 但…这位怀炎将军看起来却像一个普普通通的老爷爷。他真的是仙舟将军吗？
t_en: But... General Huaiyan looks like an ordinary old grandpa. Is he really a Xianzhou general?

t_ch: 也许他是机器人也说不定。
t_en: Perhaps he's a robot.

t_ch: 就像黑塔女士那样？唔，有道理。不管怎么看仙舟人，有一副老爷爷的外貌就很奇怪。
t_en: Like Madam Herta? Hmmm, that would make sense. Anyway, it's really weird for a Xianzhou native to look so old.

t_ch: 好问题，他就在这边，不如你亲自问他？
t_en: Good question. He's right there, so why don't you ask him yourself?

t_ch: 不要，我还想多活一阵子。
t_en: Nah, I'd like to live a little longer.

t_ch: 走吧，将军让彦卿招待我们，显然是有要事讨论。咱们就别呆在这儿耽误时间了。
t_en: We should leave. General Jing Yuan asked Yanqing to entertain us, so obviously, he has important matters to discuss. Let's not waste any time here.

t_ch: 方才彦卿出言无状，让您见笑了。
t_en: I'm sorry for Yanqing's rudeness.

t_ch: 哪里哪里，被云璃那丫头如此挤兑，就算是木人也有三分火气呢。你把弟子教得很好。呵呵，若换成老朽年轻的时候，只怕要当场翻脸一剑砍去。
t_en: Not at all. Taunted by Yunli like that, even a training dummy would be angry. You've taught your apprentice well. If it were me in my younger days, I'm afraid I'd have drawn my sword and fought.

t_ch: 彦卿小朋友已经能为你分忧解难。反倒是云璃，始终是孩子心性，对剑以外的事情一概不感兴趣。老朽此番带云璃来参加演武仪典，也是想让这孩子见见世面。
t_en: Yanqing can understand your concerns. On the other hand, Yunli is still a naive girl who's only interested in swordplay. Honestly, I brought her here to participate in the Wardance because I want to broaden her horizons.

t_ch: 这是青镞姐刚才发过来的案呈，让我看看……
t_en: This is the report Qingzu just sent me. Let me take a look...

t_ch: 将军还真是给了我一个大难题，要说服公司。
t_en: Looks like the general has given me a tough challenge. He wants me to try and help put the IPC's mind at ease.

t_ch: 这算是将军对你的考验吗？
t_en: Is this some kind of test from the general?

t_ch: 也谈不上「考验」吧。除了战术与武艺之外，云骑士官偶尔也得解决一些外交纠纷。
t_en: Well, it's not exactly a "test." As Cloud Knight officers, we not only learn the art of war and martial arts but also occasionally have to handle diplomatic disputes.

t_ch: 只是…用语言交流，终归不如战场上用武器沟通那么简单。尤其是要面对公司的人，他们说起话来总是一套一套的，最让人头疼了。
t_en: It's just, you know... Talking things out isn't as straightforward as duking it out with weapons on the battlefield. This is especially true when you're up against the IPC, with their non-stop corporate babble.

t_ch: 不操心这些了，我带各位去客栈吧？
t_en: Well, let's not worry about that for now. Shall I take you to the inn?

t_ch: 再等等。
t_en: Not yet.

t_ch: 走吧。
t_en: Let's go.

t_ch: 目送彦卿等人离去后……
t_en: After watching Yanqing and the others leave...

t_ch: 罗浮的演武仪典能得到您大驾光临，自然是罗浮的荣幸。
t_en: It is the Luofu's honor to have you in attendance at the Wardance.

t_ch: 只是，区区一个演武仪典，却能劳动「朱明」和「曜青」的两位天将同时到来，恐怕也不仅仅是为了观礼吧？炎老这次前来，元帅可有什么吩咐？
t_en: Yet, the fact that a simple martial arts ceremony has attracted esteemed generals from the Zhuming and the Yaoqing implies intentions beyond mere spectating. Might there be any specific instructions from the Marshal?

t_ch: 景元，你多心啦。我说过，这次前来除了让孩子见见世面，老朽对罗浮的状况并无说三道四的想法。
t_en: You're overthinking it, Jing Yuan. As I said, I'm here to broaden my granddaughter's horizons. I have no ulterior motives.

t_ch: 但那位曜青将军有什么打算，同样也不是老夫能说三道四的。
t_en: However, I have no clue what the Yaoqing Arbiter-General has in mind.

t_ch: 还记得吗？在你履任之初，我曾告诉过你，「帝弓天将的战场不仅在仙舟之外」。上阵折冲，对内斡旋…将军这个名头所承载的重量要远胜于它的字面意思。
t_en: Do you remember when you accepted this position? I told you that an Arbiter-General's battlefield goes beyond the physical one. You'll need to lead and manage everything on the Xianzhou... The title of Arbiter-General holds a weight far greater than its literal meaning.

t_ch: 那么多年过去了，你始终做得很好。但对仙舟人来说，活得太久是一种诅咒。这意味着生命中犯下的每一次错误都在暗处虎视眈眈，终有一日追上你，吞没你。
t_en: So many years have passed, and you've done well. However, longevity for the Xianzhou people can be a curse. Living too long means that every mistake you made will lurk in the shadows, and one day, they'll eventually catch up to you.

t_ch: {RUBY_B#建木灾异始末}罗浮上发生的一切{RUBY_E#}，元帅皆已知悉。而曜青的天击将军……
t_en: The Marshal knows {RUBY_B#The ins and outs of the Ambrosial Arbor Disaster}everything that has happened on the Luofu{RUBY_E#}. As for the Merlin's Claw of the Yaoqing...

t_ch: …她正是为你而来。
t_en: ...She has come specifically for you.

t_ch: 不过怪了，她怎么还没来？都说曜青的天击将军一贯动如雷霆，先声夺人，今天这么迟到可不是她的作风啊。
t_en: Speaking of which, why hasn't she arrived yet? They say the Merlin's Claw strikes like lightning. Being late isn't her style.

t_ch: 怀炎将军此言差矣，敝上一早就到了。只是两位想必也有所耳闻，她的性情向来不受拘束。
t_en: That's not true, General Huaiyan. She's been here a while, but I'm sure you've heard of her unbridled nature.

t_ch: 她一下星槎就跑了个没影，说有事要办，拦都拦不住。
t_en: As soon as she disembarked from the starskiff, she mentioned having something to attend to and simply disappeared.

t_ch: 想必两位就是仙舟「曜青」的使者了。
t_en: You must be the messengers from the Xianzhou Yaoqing, I assume.

t_ch: 天击将军帐下幕僚，椒丘、貊泽，拜见两位天将。
t_en: We are Jiaoqiu and Moze, retainers of the Merlin's Claw. It's an honor to meet you in person, Arbiter-Generals.

t_ch: 有意思，客人到访不径直来见主人，反倒是派人传信。这又是唱的哪一出？你说说，她有什么比来这儿更重要的事情啊？
t_en: Now, this is interesting. A guest who doesn't come to visit but sends a message instead. What does she mean? Tell me, what could be more important to her than coming here?

t_ch: 敝上听说，罗浮之上鳞渊境中最近多了一处奇景，十分壮观，想来是赏景去了。
t_en: Master heard about a spectacular view in Scalegorge Waterscape. I believe she went there to enjoy it.

t_ch: 好个「奇景」。我来给你翻译翻译，景元，这小子是在阴阳怪气你呢。
t_en: A "spectacular" view? Hah! Did you hear that, Jing Yuan? This person is being sarcastic.

t_ch: 怀炎将军言重了，在下不过是据实传达罢了。我家将军考虑到让两位久等不妥，先遣我们二人前来……
t_en: Please do not misunderstand me, General Huaiyan. I was simply stating the truth. Master thought it would be inappropriate to keep you waiting, so she sent us here...

t_ch: 待她赏景结束，便会亲自到访，向两位致歉。
t_en: Once she's finished with admiring the scenery, she'll personally come and apologize to the both of you.

t_ch: 与此同时，司辰宫外…
t_en: In the meantime, outside the Palace of Astrum...

t_ch: 糟糕，忘了让云璃把剑还我了……
t_en: Drat, I forgot to ask Yunli to return my sword...

t_ch: 我去帮你要回来吧。
t_en: I'll go get it back for you.

t_ch: 不必麻烦了。朱明的人一时半刻也不会离开罗浮，之后见面再讨要也不迟。
t_en: No need to bother. The Zhuming envoys won't be leaving the Luofu anytime soon. I'll ask for it later when we meet again.

t_ch: 这把剑怕是要不回来了！
t_en: I don't think you'll ever get your sword back!

t_ch: 那可不行！每把剑都是彦卿的宝贝！
t_en: Unacceptable! Every sword is precious to me!

t_ch: 若不是将军阻拦就凭那女孩大言不惭的口气，我一定要让她好好领教一番我的剑法…
t_en: If it weren't for the general's interference, I would have taught that shameless girl a lesson in swordplay...

t_ch: 说起来，不知是不是错觉，我总觉得今天的将军有些…唔，有些拘谨？是因为那位怀炎老先生的到访吗？
t_en: By the way, I don't know if it's just me, but the general seemed a little... hmmm, reserved? Could it be because of Elder Huaiyan's visit?

t_ch: 拘谨？有吗？
t_en: Reserved? Really?

t_ch: 也许是彦卿多心了。
t_en: Maybe I'm just overthinking things.

t_ch: 你并没有多心。进司辰宫开始，我才意识到你所说的朱明仙舟的使者竟是朱明的将军本人。那么，曜青仙舟的使者，想必也就是那位天击将军了？
t_en: No, you're not. When I entered the Palace of Astrum, I realized that the messenger from the Xianzhou Zhuming was actually the Arbiter-General himself. So, the messenger from the Xianzhou Yaoqing must be the Merlin's Claw herself, I presume?

t_ch: 没错。
t_en: That's right.
